gloss.en: a feeling of worry or fear

words.example: She felt deep anxiety before the exam.

gloss.en: feeling worried or nervous

words.example: The bus was late and Sue began to get anxious.

gloss.en: a statement saying sorry for something wrong

words.example: He made a sincere apology for his mistake.

gloss.en: the punctuation mark ’ used in writing

words.example: You need an apostrophe in the word "don't".

gloss.en: easy to see or understand

words.example: Their devotion was apparent.

gloss.en: according to what you have heard or seen

words.example: Apparently they are getting divorced soon.

gloss.en: an urgent public request for help or money

words.example: The charity launched an appeal for donations.

gloss.en: a person who formally applies for something

words.example: There were over 500 applicants for the job.

gloss.en: feeling or showing thanks and gratitude

words.example: She gave an appreciative smile for the gift.

gloss.en: a way of dealing with something

words.example: She took the wrong approach in her dealings with them.

gloss.en: suitable or correct for the situation

words.example: It is not an appropriate time to talk.

gloss.en: in a suitable or correct way

words.example: Please dress appropriately for the meeting.

gloss.en: a positive opinion that something is good

words.example: She desperately wanted to win her father's approval.

gloss.en: to think something is good or acceptable

words.example: I told my mother I wanted to leave school but she didn't approve.

gloss.en: close to but not exact

words.example: The approximate cost is fifty dollars.

gloss.en: a curved structure forming the top of a passage

words.example: We walked under the stone arch.

gloss.en: a person who studies ancient cultures and objects

words.example: The archaeologist found an old gold coin.

gloss.en: a person who studies ancient cultures and objects

words.example: The archeologist dug up an ancient pot.

gloss.en: very cold; of the far north

words.example: The wind was bitterly arctic that night.

gloss.en: to happen or start to exist

words.example: An opportunity arose to work in the United States.

gloss.en: people of the highest social class

words.example: The aristocracy owned most of the land.

gloss.en: carrying or using weapons

words.example: The police caught an armed robber.

gloss.en: as much as you can hold in your arms

words.example: She carried an armful of books home.

gloss.en: weapons used by armies

words.example: The soldiers carried arms and ammunition.

gloss.en: to cause a feeling or reaction

words.example: His strange behaviour aroused our suspicion.

gloss.en: thinking you are better than others

words.example: He is too arrogant to admit his mistakes.

gloss.en: a thin pointed stick shot from a bow

words.example: He shot an arrow at the target.

gloss.en: a tube carrying blood from the heart

words.example: The doctor checked the blocked artery.

gloss.en: made by people, not natural

words.example: She wore an artificial flower in her hair.

gloss.en: in a way that is not natural

words.example: The fruit was artificially coloured.

gloss.en: connected with art or artists

words.example: We admired the artistic works of the period.

gloss.en: great skill in making art

words.example: The painting shows real artistry.

gloss.en: pictures and images made for printing

words.example: Can you finish the artwork for the poster by Friday?

gloss.en: feeling shame or embarrassment

words.example: She was deeply ashamed of her behaviour at the party.

gloss.en: to one side; out of the way

words.example: She pulled the curtain aside.

gloss.en: a particular part or feature of something

words.example: The book aims to cover all aspects of city life.

gloss.en: to judge the quality or value of something

words.example: Teachers assess the children's progress each term.

gloss.en: a careful judgement about something

words.example: They made a detailed assessment of the risks.

gloss.en: a valuable or useful person or thing

words.example: In his job, patience is an invaluable asset.

gloss.en: to give someone a task or duty

words.example: The teacher assigned a different task to each child.

gloss.en: help or support

words.example: They offered technical assistance to the team.

gloss.en: to connect things or people in your mind

words.example: I always associate the smell of baking with my childhood.

gloss.en: connected with something else

words.example: He warned of the risks associated with taking drugs.

gloss.en: a group of people joined for a purpose

words.example: Do you belong to any trade association?

gloss.en: to think something is true without proof

words.example: It is reasonable to assume the economy will improve.

gloss.en: a belief that something is true without proof

words.example: We made the assumption that prices would rise.

gloss.en: to tell someone something is definitely true

words.example: I assure you that I did not do it deliberately.

gloss.en: to surprise someone very much

gloss.en: very surprised

words.example: I was astonished to see her there.

gloss.en: very surprising; hard to believe

words.example: She ran the race in an astonishing time.

gloss.en: a feeling of great surprise

words.example: To my astonishment, the door was open.

gloss.en: the study of stars and planets

words.example: He studies astronomy at university.

gloss.en: sports such as running and jumping

words.example: She is very good at athletics.

gloss.en: joined to or fond of something

words.example: She is very attached to her old dog.

gloss.en: a file sent with an email

words.example: No attachment was included in the email.

gloss.en: able to be achieved

words.example: Set goals that are realistic and attainable.

gloss.en: the act of achieving something

words.example: The school is proud of its high attainment.

gloss.en: an act of trying to do something

words.example: I passed my driving test at the first attempt.

gloss.en: listening or watching carefully

words.example: The students were quiet and attentive.

gloss.en: in a careful, focused way

words.example: She listened attentively to the teacher.

gloss.en: the quality of being pleasing or appealing

gloss.en: not typical or usual

words.example: This warm weather is atypical for winter.

gloss.en: a purple vegetable; eggplant

words.example: She cooked an aubergine for dinner.

gloss.en: a sale where things go to the highest bidder

gloss.en: connected with recorded sound

words.example: He saved the audio files on his phone.

gloss.en: a short performance to test an actor or singer

gloss.en: to give official permission for something

gloss.en: trusted and respected as accurate

words.example: This is the most authoritative book on the subject.

gloss.en: working by itself without a person

words.example: The shop has automatic doors.

gloss.en: by itself without a person operating it

words.example: The heating switches off automatically.

gloss.en: knowing that something exists or matters

words.example: We need greater awareness of healthy eating.

gloss.en: a feeling of deep respect and wonder

words.example: We stood in awe before the huge mountain.

gloss.en: making you feel embarrassed or uneasy

words.example: There was an awkward silence.

gloss.en: a tool with a blade for chopping wood

words.example: He used an ax to cut the wood.

gloss.en: a tool with a blade for chopping wood

words.example: She swung the axe at the log.

gloss.en: a line around which something turns

words.example: The Earth spins on its axis.

gloss.en: located at the rear of something

words.example: We were sitting in the back row.

gloss.en: towards a place that is behind

words.example: He took a step backward in surprise.

gloss.en: towards a position that is behind

words.example: I lost my balance and fell backwards.

gloss.en: an area of ground behind a house

words.example: The children played in the backyard.

gloss.en: tiny living things that can cause disease

words.example: Freezing does not kill all bacteria.

gloss.en: easily annoyed or angry

words.example: He gets bad-tempered when he is tired.

gloss.en: a small sign worn to show something

gloss.en: fair and showing all sides equally

words.example: The report gave a balanced view of both sides.

gloss.en: unwilling to move or work properly

gloss.en: a slow song that tells a story

words.example: The singer performed a sad ballad.

gloss.en: a graceful dance that tells a story

words.example: She wants to be a ballet dancer.

gloss.en: a thin rubber bag filled with air or gas

words.example: The child blew up a red balloon.

gloss.en: an official rule that forbids something

words.example: The city introduced a smoking ban.

gloss.en: a person who works at a high level in a bank

gloss.en: unable to pay what you owe

words.example: The company went bankrupt last year.

gloss.en: the state of being unable to pay debts

gloss.en: a place where you buy and drink drinks

gloss.en: a place where men get their hair cut

gloss.en: only just; almost not

gloss.en: a thing bought for less than usual

gloss.en: land where plants cannot grow

gloss.en: in the most important ways; essentially

gloss.en: a public building for bathing

gloss.en: a small dog with short legs used for hunting

gloss.en: a large wild animal with thick fur

words.example: We saw a black bear in the forest.

gloss.en: an effect or influence on something

gloss.en: a regular sound or rhythm

gloss.en: to make something more beautiful

words.example: They planted flowers to beautify the park.

gloss.en: to ask for something in an anxious way

gloss.en: the state of existing

gloss.en: to shout in a deep, loud voice

gloss.en: a curve in a road or river
